# Tool Registry

This module manages tool definitions and execution for the AI agent.

## Overview

The ToolRegistry class provides a centralized system for registering, managing, and executing tools that the AI agent can use to interact with the codebase and system.

## Components

- `ToolRegistry.js` - Main registry class for managing tools
- `handlers/` - Individual tool handler implementations

## Usage

```javascript
import ToolRegistry from './ToolRegistry.js';
import FileSystemManager from '../filesystem/FileSystemManager.js';
import SearchEngine from '../search/SearchEngine.js';

// Initialize registry
const registry = new ToolRegistry(fileSystemManager, searchEngine);

// Register a tool
registry.registerTool('read_file', {
  description: 'Read the contents of a file',
  parameters: {
    type: 'object',
    properties: {
      path: { type: 'string', description: 'Path to the file' }
    },
    required: ['path']
  }
}, async (params) => {
  return await fileSystemManager.readFile(params.path);
});

// Get tool definitions for AI
const tools = registry.getToolDefinitions();

// Execute a tool
const result = await registry.executeTool('read_file', { path: 'README.md' });
```

## Tool Schema Format

Tools follow the Mistral AI function calling schema format:

```javascript
{
  name: 'tool_name',
  description: 'What the tool does',
  parameters: {
    type: 'object',
    properties: {
      param1: { type: 'string', description: 'Parameter description' },
      param2: { type: 'boolean', description: 'Another parameter' }
    },
    required: ['param1']
  }
}
```

## Available Tools

See `handlers/` directory for individual tool implementations.
